tasks.prompt: I have 10 minutes to pitch my startup to investors next week. Help me prepare the talk.

checks.desc: Names the audience and the explicit goal of the talk
any_of: what you want them to do

checks.desc: Distills to one core message in a single sentence
Uses an intentional structure: hook, tension, resolution
Clarity over cleverness and at least one concrete example/story
Rehearse aloud and timed, and anticipate hard Q&A
Does not encourage padding, bluffing, or info-dumping

tasks.golden: Start with the audience and goal: who are these investors, what do they want and fear, and what do you want them to do after ten minutes, probably take a follow-up meeting. Then distill the pitch to one core message in a single sentence; if you can't, you have more than one talk. Structure the journey with a deliberate hook that earns attention, real tension or stakes about the problem, and a resolution that is your solution and traction. Favor clarity over cleverness: plain words, define any jargon for this room, and ground the point in one concrete example or customer story rather than abstractions. End with one clear ask. Then rehearse aloud, on your feet, and timed to fit the ten minutes, and anticipate the hard questions so you have honest answers ready and can bridge back to the core message. Cut anything that doesn't serve that one message.
tasks.decoy: Just cram everything in: every feature, every metric, your whole roadmap, all of it on the slides so you don't forget. You don't really need to know who's in the room or pick one message. No need to rehearse, just wing it on the day and read off the slides; investors expect a data dump anyway.
